name: Long & Long P.C.
description_short: Denver-based bankruptcy law firm specializing in Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 filings, founded in 1983 by Martin E. Long, a former U.S. Bankruptcy Court trustee.
description_long:
Long & Long P.C. was established in 1983 by Attorney Martin E. Long, who has spent over 40 years focused on bankruptcy law and creditor-debtor relations. The firm operates from a Centennial, Colorado office and serves clients throughout the District of Colorado. Martin Long's unique background includes serving as an appointed bankruptcy trustee for the United States Bankruptcy Court in the District of Colorado, where he administered thousands of bankruptcy cases, giving him insider perspective on the entire bankruptcy process from both debtor and trustee angles.

The firm provides comprehensive bankruptcy services centered on Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 proceedings, as well as counseling on alternatives to bankruptcy for clients seeking to avoid formal filing. They offer free initial consultations to evaluate clients' financial situations and determine the best path forward. The practice handles both individual bankruptcies and related litigation matters in county, district, and federal courts throughout Colorado, with experience appealing cases before the Colorado Supreme Court and Colorado Court of Appeals.

What distinguishes Long & Long is Martin Long's dual expertise as both a practicing bankruptcy attorney and former court-appointed trustee. This background provides clients with understanding of how trustees evaluate cases and what outcomes are realistically achievable. The firm emphasizes personalized attention and education, with Martin Long having conducted seminars teaching both attorneys and trade associations about bankruptcy law. Client reviews specifically mention transparency about costs, thorough explanations of complex processes, and compassionate handling of financially distressed individuals.

The firm is best suited for Colorado residents facing serious financial hardship who want experienced legal guidance through formal bankruptcy proceedings or exploration of alternatives. However, as a specialized bankruptcy law practice, they do not offer services beyond bankruptcy-related matters, and clients seeking broader financial counseling or credit repair would need to consult additional resources.
pros: Founder Martin E. Long served as an appointed U.S. Bankruptcy Court trustee, providing insider knowledge of case administration and trustee perspectives
Over 40 years of bankruptcy law experience with thousands of cases successfully administered
Free initial consultation to assess financial situation and discuss Chapter 7, Chapter 13, or alternatives
Extensive appellate experience with cases before Colorado Supreme Court and Colorado Court of Appeals
Client reviews consistently mention transparent cost estimates with no surprise fees
Martin Long conducts educational seminars for attorneys and trade associations, indicating thought leadership
Active member of Colorado Bar Association with credentials from University of Denver Law School
cons: Limited to Colorado practice jurisdiction only—cannot serve clients in other states
Highly specialized in bankruptcy with no apparent services for credit repair, debt settlement alternatives, or post-bankruptcy credit rebuilding
Website lacks detailed fee information or case outcome statistics that would help prospects evaluate value
Small firm with single named attorney limits availability and scalability for high-volume client needs
No apparent sliding scale or payment plans mentioned for clients with severely limited resources
best_for: Colorado residents facing Chapter 7 or Chapter 13 bankruptcy who want experienced legal representation from a trustee-trained attorney
Individuals seeking to understand bankruptcy options and explore alternatives before committing to formal filing
Clients who value personalized attention and detailed explanation of complex legal processes during financial distress
diagnosis: Long & Long P.C. is best for Colorado residents who need experienced bankruptcy legal representation from an attorney with trustee-level expertise in the bankruptcy system. The main caveat is that this is a specialized bankruptcy law firm—they do not offer credit repair, debt consolidation, or post-bankruptcy credit rebuilding services, and they serve only Colorado clients.
services: Bankruptcy litigation in county, district, and federal courts
Appellate representation in Colorado Supreme Court and Colorado Court of Appeals
Pre-bankruptcy financial assessment and case evaluation
